visual_features.overall_style: Clean, conservative, content-first layout with a light, corporate–tourism feel. Strongly minimalist: no background imagery in this view, mostly white canvas with a soft nautical blue brand color. It feels slightly dated (pre-material design) but simple and unobtrusive.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is created primarily through typography (large centered H1, smaller left-aligned body copy) and spacing. The logo and top navigation bar form the first row; the large centered page title 'NEMUNAS TRAVEL, JSC' is the main focal point due to size and central alignment. Below that, paragraphs of body text form a long, single-column narrative. Inline blue links inside paragraphs create secondary focal points.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous vertical whitespace between the navigation bar and the page title, and between the title and the first paragraph. Side margins are moderate, giving a relatively wide text column but still leaving comfortable margins. Within paragraphs, line spacing is moderate; the overall feeling is open rather than crowded.
unique_elements: Soft diamond-shaped logo containing wave forms suggesting a river or water motif
Very minimal hero area: no imagery, only a centered typographic title with large vertical padding
Language switcher using small country flags aligned to the right of the header
Simple text links used for most interactions (including the phone number and FAQ), with no visible buttons in the primary content area

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Single centered content column likely based on a 12‑column grid with a max-width container around 960–1140px.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Top fixed-width header with logo on left and horizontal navigation on right, followed by a full-width centered title and a single-column text block.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Header section (logo + navigation) at the top, then a large vertical gap, then a content section consisting of one large heading and a stack of paragraphs. No visible sidebars or secondary columns on this view.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Logo left-aligned in header; navigation items right-aligned within the same horizontal row. Main title is center-aligned. Body text below is left-aligned within a centered content column.
responsive_hints: Content is contained in a central column with ample side margins, suggesting a responsive container rather than a fixed-width page.
Navigation text is spaced horizontally with no wrapping visible; likely collapses to a stacked or hamburger menu on smaller screens.
Phone number and language flags on the far right may wrap below nav or be hidden behind a mobile menu at smaller breakpoints.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. Header bar with logo and nav forms a thin horizontal strip at the very top. Below it, a large vertical gap creates a pseudo-hero area dominated by the centered H1. Under the title, multiple paragraphs of text are stacked vertically in a single column, stretching nearly the full width of the central content area. Inline links within paragraphs are styled as blue text without additional structure.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport is used by the header and the large blank space above the main title. The central title and initial paragraph occupy the next 20–30%, with the remaining vertical space filled by stacked text. No horizontal space is used for secondary columns; most horizontal real estate is given to the text column, with margins of approximately 10–15% on each side.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Low to medium density. Paragraphs are reasonably long, but vertical spacing between major elements (header, title, text block) is generous. Within the text block, paragraphs are set close enough to feel like continuous reading rather than segmented cards. The overall impression is an editorial, brochure-like page rather than a dashboard or booking interface.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Strong vertical flow. The eye starts at the top-left logo, moves horizontally across the header to the navigation and phone number, then drops down to the large centered page title. From there, the flow continues straight down through the paragraphs, with occasional lateral attention shifts to blue inline links. The language flags and reCAPTCHA badge are tertiary focal points at the far right edges.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No primary buttons are visible in this particular view; interactive elements are simple text links (blue, no background, likely underlined on hover). Based on the aesthetic, any buttons would likely be rectangular with slightly rounded corners (2–4px radius), flat (no heavy shadows), primary blue background (#3B88E2) with white text, and medium padding (e.g., 10–12px vertical, 20–24px horizontal).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure is semantic <button> elements for actions and <a> elements styled as buttons for navigation or external links, possibly wrapped in a container div for layout and spacing if multiple buttons appear side by side.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No cards are visible on this screen. Given the flat, minimalist style, cards would likely use a white background on a slightly off-white page background, with a 1px light gray border (#E0E4EA) and possibly a very subtle shadow (0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)). Corners would be slightly rounded (4px) with internal padding of 16–24px and a simple vertical stack of title, subtitle, and content.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical card structure would be a container <div class='card'> with nested elements for image (optional), heading (<h3> or <h4>), metadata, and body text. Multiple cards would be arranged in a responsive flex or grid layout (<div class='card-grid'> with display:flex or display:grid).

ui_patterns.visual_description: No custom form fields are visible except a standard Google reCAPTCHA badge. Based on the rest of the design, inputs would be full-width fields with a light border (#D0D5DD), 4px radius, and 10–12px internal padding. Labels likely sit above the inputs, left-aligned. Error messages would probably appear as small red text under the input, with the border changing to the error color (#C62828). Overall form density would be moderate: stacked fields with 12–16px vertical spacing.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms would use a stacked layout: <form> containing a series of <label> elements followed by <input>, <select>, or <textarea>, each wrapped in a form-group <div>. Fieldsets could be used to group logical sections (e.g., passenger details, contact info). Submit actions as <button type='submit'>.

navigation.visual_description: A horizontal top navigation bar. On the left, a logo mark and logotype; on the right, main nav links in uppercase (HOME, BOATS, TOURS, etc.), evenly spaced with generous letterspacing. Text is dark gray/black by default, with the active page (ABOUT US) in brand blue (#3B88E2). One nav item ('BOATS') has a small downward arrow, indicating a dropdown submenu. Off to the far right, a blue phone number and a 'F.A.Q.' text link, followed by small flag icons representing language selection.

list.visual_description: No explicit lists are shown; information is presented as continuous paragraphs. Where lists appear elsewhere, they would likely be simple vertical lists with minimal styling: bullet or dash markers, left-aligned, with moderate line spacing and no borders. Thumbnail or icon use would be minimal, in keeping with the clean style.
list.structural_pattern: Standard semantic HTML lists: <ul> or <ol> with <li> items. For more complex 'media' lists (e.g., tours with images), a flexbox 'media object' pattern would be expected: an image on the left and a text block on the right, wrapped in a <li> or <div class='list-item'>.

heading.visual_style: Uppercase, modern sans-serif headings with relatively light weight and generous letterspacing, giving a clean, airy, corporate feel.

body.visual_style: Neutral, readable sans-serif with medium x-height and low contrast strokes, optimized for continuous reading.

font_families.code: Source Code Pro, Menlo, Monaco, monospace

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical interactive components (buttons, inputs, small cards) would use 8–16px internal padding (e.g., 8px vertical, 16px horizontal for buttons; 10–12px all around for inputs).
spacing_system.section_margin: Major sections (header to title, title to content blocks) appear to use 48–64px vertical spacing to create clear separation.

interaction_hints: Text links and navigation items likely change to the brand blue (#3B88E2) on hover, with an underline or subtle color shift; the active nav link is persistently blue.
Dropdown indicator (chevron) next to 'BOATS' suggests a hover-activated or click-activated dropdown menu with a simple vertical list of links.
Phone number is styled in blue, implying click-to-call on mobile; hover likely underlines or darkens.
Language flags act as clickable switches; hover probably adds a border or slight background tint.
Page transitions and interactions are likely minimal and instantaneous; if animations are added, they should be subtle, such as 150–200ms color transitions and gentle fade/slide for dropdown menus.
